title: High quality DNA extraction protocol from recalcitrant plant tissues
author: Todd GB McLay
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.i8jchun

steps.instruction: %s %s
%s %s Mix on a stir plate (unheated) until the solution is clear.
Make 100x (=4%) BSA from powder in H 2 O. Combine 5M NaCl with 100x BSA in a 5:1 ratio. This can be frozen for long-term storage.
Add 600 mL of CTAB per sample to a falcon tube. In fumehood, add 2 μL of BME per mL of CTAB, and 4 μL of Proteinase K per reaction. Warm at 65 °C.

Grind 20-50 mg of plant tissue using your favourite grinding method. I use liquid nitrogen and grinding sand for particularly sclerophyllous tissue. Place into labelled 1.7 mL eppendorf tube. At this stage it is useful to label two additional tubes per sample. You could either label them now or later during a centrifugation step.
Add 1 mL of STE to tissue. Vortex thoroughly so tissue is fully mixed. Spin at 5000 rpm for 10 minutes. Discard supernatent by pouring off.

In a fumehood, add 500 μL of the pre-warmed CTAB solution to each tube. Add 100 uL of NaCl:BSA solution. Vortex. Incubate at 65 C for at least an hour (to overnight). Mix by vortexing or shaking several times through incubation. 01:00:00, CTAB incubation (minimum)

Add 450 μL of chloroform to each tube. *If this volume of chloroform will not fit into the eppendorf tube, split the tissue+CTAB mixture into two separate eppendorf tubes and add 450 μL of chloroform to each tube. Shake to mix and open tube to release built-up gas using a kimwipe to catch drops. Be careful of cross-contamination at this point. Spin at maximum speed for 5 minutes. 00:05:00, centrifugation
using a P200 set to 190 μL, remove the top aqueous phase (usually between 300-400 μL) and put into a new eppendorf tube. Be careful not to include any of the solid layer. a

Add 450 μL to the removed aqueous phase solution. Shake to mix and open tube to release built-up gas using a kimwipe to catch drops. Spin at maximum speed for 5 minutes. 00:05:00, Second centrifugation

Using a P200 set to 190 μL, remove the top aqueous phase (usually between 300-400 μL) and put into a new eppendorf tube. Be even more vigilant at this step as there is no solid barrier separating the top aqueous phase from the chloroform.

Add 500 uL of room temperature isopropanol. Mix by gently inverting five times. *At this step it is possible to add 110 μL sodium acetate (3M, pH5.2), 500 μL isopropanol, and mix by gently inverting with a 20 mins incubation at room temp. Then follow Steps 13-16. This avoids the need to precipitate again with NaAC after re-eluting the DNA (Steps 17-20) . However, this step did not work with some taxa and resulted in loss of DNA. 00:20:00, DNA precipitation

Spin for 10 minutes at maximum speed with eppendorf tubes facing inwards (i.e. the hinge is to the outside of the centre of the centrifuge). A DNA pellet may appear at the bottom of the tube. a Carefully pour off the supernatant.

Add 500 μL of 70% ethanol (made fresh). Try to dislodge the pellet from the bottom of the tube through pipetting or flicking the tube. Centrifuge for five minutes at maximum speed. Carefully pour off the ethanol. 00:05:00, Ethanol centrifugation

Dry pellets for at least two hours to overnight by leaving tubes open on bench/in fumehood, with kimwipes securely placed over them. Alternatively, you can wick the remaining ethanol away using kimwipes. 02:00:00, Drying pellet (minimum).
Add 50 μL of 10mM Tris-HCl pH8 and allow DNA to resuspend for at least one hour before use.

Add 200 μL of 100% ethanol and 36.25 μL of NaAC (2.4M) to each tube. Mix by inverting. Centrifuge at maximum speed for 10 minutes. *this step can be incorporated into the isopropanol step by including NaAC so the final concentration is 0.3M, although this did not seem to work for Xanthorrhoea . 00:10:00, Sodium acetate centrifugation

Carefully pour off supernatant. Occassionally the pellet is not stuck to the tube so careful it doesn't come out. Add 1000 μL of 70% ethanol. Try to dislodge the pellet from the bottom of the tube through pipetting or flicking the tube. Centrifuge at maximum speed for three minutes. 00:03:00, Ethanol wash centrifugation
Carefully pour off supernatant. Dry pellets for at least two hours to overnight by leaving tubes open on bench/in fumehood, with kimwipes securely placed over them. 02:00:00, Drying (minimum).
Add 50-100 μL of 10mM Tris-HCl pH8 and allow DNA to resuspend for at least one hour before use. Check quality and quantity using spectrophotometry.
